articles.title: VEO 2 IS COMING TO YOUTUBE SHORTS
articles.summary: YouTube has integrated DeepMind's Veo 2 into Dream Screen, enabling users to generate high-quality AI video clips from text prompts.

articles.title: ADOBE FIREFLY VIDEO MODEL
articles.summary: Adobe introduces the Firefly Video Model, offering IP-friendly, commercially safe generative AI tools for video and audio creation.

articles.title: GOOGLE AI AND WOMEN'S CANCER RESEARCH
articles.summary: Google has partnered with the Institute of Women's Cancers to enhance AI-driven cancer research, focusing on improving outcomes for breast and gynecological cancers.

articles.title: MIXTURE OF EXPERTS FOR SPECULATIVE DECODING
articles.summary: Jakiro enhances speculative decoding by using Mixture of Experts to generate diverse predictions, reducing correlation among candidates and improving inference speed.

articles.title: BACKDOOR ATTACKS IN CLIP
articles.summary: Researchers reveal that CLIP models are highly vulnerable to poisoning backdoor attacks, achieving nearly 100% attack success with minimal poisoned data. They propose an efficient detection method using local outlier detection, uncovering unintentional backdoors in existing datasets.

articles.title: MOBILELLM (HUGGING FACE HUB)
articles.summary: Meta has released checkpoints for their small but mighty mobile friendly language models.

articles.title: LLMS WITH FEWER PARAMETERS
articles.summary: Researchers demonstrate that LLMs can maintain learning capacity while reducing non-embedding parameters by up to 77%. By replacing dense layers with optimized subnetworks, they achieve comparable performance with significantly fewer resources.

articles.title: BETTER REASONING WITH MASK-ENHANCED AUTOREGRESSIVE PREDICTION
articles.summary: MEAP integrates Masked Language Modeling into Next-Token Prediction using a decoder-only Transformer. By masking a small fraction of input tokens, it improves information retrieval tasks while maintaining reasoning capabilities.

articles.title: COMPLEX FUNCTION BENCHMARKS FOR LLMS
articles.summary: This repository contains benchmarks and tools for assessing AI models on complex function calls.

articles.title: BILLIONAIRES TALK AUTOMATING JOBS AWAY
articles.summary: OpenAI is collaborating with SoftBank to automate white-collar workflows using AI, sparking concerns about large-scale unemployment. OpenAI also launched a new AI agent for research and the o3-mini reasoning model, while the EU implemented a ban on risky AI systems. Additionally, ByteDance's YuE model generates music from prompts, and Anthropic introduces Constitutional Classifiers to enhance AI safety measures.

articles.title: WHY AI IS A PHILOSOPHICAL RUPTURE
articles.summary: Tobias Rees argues that generative AI challenges long-standing human-machine distinctions, presenting new forms of intelligence that require philosophical reconsideration. AI's ability to operate beyond traditional comprehension models suggests its potential to transform human cognition and self-awareness. Rees advocates for philosophical engagement in AI development to explore and navigate this new terrain of human-AI symbiosis.

articles.title: EU PUTS OUT GUIDANCE ON USES OF AI THAT ARE BANNED UNDER ITS AI ACT
articles.summary: The EU's AI Act compliance deadline has passed, prompting the Commission to release guidance for developers on adhering to new AI regulations. Breaches of prohibited use cases, such as social scoring, could incur severe penalties. The guidelines aim to provide clarity but are not legally binding. Formal adoption is pending translation.

articles.title: OPENAI UPDATES MODEL SPEC
articles.summary: OpenAI has released an updated Model Spec document under a CC0 license, reinforcing principles of transparency, customizability, and intellectual freedom while maintaining safety guardrails.

articles.title: DEEP RESEARCH COMES TO GEMINI ANDROID APP
articles.summary: Deep Research, Gemini's first agentic feature, is now available for Android, enabling multi-step web research with customizable plans.

articles.title: DEEPSEEK GETS AN 'F' IN SAFETY FROM RESEARCHERS
articles.summary: Cisco tested DeepSeek's open-source model, DeepSeek R1, which failed to fend off all 50 harmful behavior prompts from the HarmBench dataset.
